feedback.text: Student of the Jean Monnet Modules project “DERECHO”: ‘Defense of European Rights and Excellence for Consumers HHarmonisation’, No 101239437. Project financed by the European Union. Jean Monnet Actions in the field of Higher Education: Modules. Call for tenders ERASMUS-JMO-2025-HEI-TCH-RSCH. In today’s society, the digital world is part of our everyday life or not. For this reason, it is very important that such laws are drafted that seek to protect us by consumers when making any kind of online movement. In my view, such regulations make it possible for all consumers, regardless of their age, origin or personal circumstances, to have the same guarantees of protection and the same sense of safety with regard to the digital world. I also believe that this initiative is necessary not only for vulnerable groups (such as older people, people with disabilities or minors), but also for everyone who has access to the network. I say that, although, for example, my generation has grown with technology already as an area of our lives, there are still situations and risks that we do not have to know how to handle, and to which ones, this law offers us the protection we need. Therefore, and to conclude, I am of the opinion that this regulation, together with any other regulation that provides consumer protection and safety against the still unknown digital world, will never be more and should be regarded as a priority.
